explicit_text: I was born and raised in the Philippines, specifically in San Pablo City, Laguna. My life there was very happy with my two loving parents and three siblings. Even though I left the Philippines in 2012 to join my husband in the United States, I still miss my father a lot. Leaving the Philippines was difficult, but I wanted to build a better future for my children.

explicit_text: I came to the United States on August 5, 2012, with my three children after being petitioned by my husband who had immigrated five years earlier. The journey was long and challenging, with stops in Japan, Hawaii, and San Francisco before finally arriving in Minneapolis. I am here to provide a better future and education for my children, even though I have not worked for the past two years and am currently focusing on improving my English.

cues: journey through Japan, Hawaii, San Francisco

explicit_text: My sister-in-law works at Delta Air Lines, and we used her itinerary when we traveled from the Philippines to the United States. Because of her connection, she was able to pick us up and help us with the flights. However, we still faced delays, like being stranded for seven hours in Japan as standby passengers.

explicit_text: When we flew to Hawaii, we were able to meet my brother and his family, which was a joyful reunion during our long journey. We spent one night with them, ate at Max's Restaurant, and went to the mall with my nephews and niece. My children really missed their cousins, so this stop was very special for us.
